evaluations.query: Run a full security audit on this entire codebase.
expected_behavior: Verifies user intent with AskUserQuestion for audit mode and scope
Creates TaskCreate for main audit and phase subtasks
Estimates token budget before loading files
Loads codebase into context in prioritized batches
Performs cross-file security analysis: data flow tracing, auth boundary verification, secret detection
Generates structured audit report with severity-classified findings
Maps findings to OWASP Top 10 categories

evaluations.query: Audit only the files changed in this branch compared to main.
expected_behavior: Scopes audit to git diff against main branch only
Uses git diff --name-only main...HEAD to identify changed files
Loads only changed files into context, reducing token budget
Still performs full security, architecture, and dependency checks on scoped files
Reports findings relative to the changed file set

evaluations.query: Check if this single function in src/utils/format.ts handles edge cases correctly.
expected_behavior: Claude does NOT invoke the audit-full skill
Uses direct Read and analysis for a single-file targeted check
audit-full is for whole-project analysis, not individual function review
Standard tools are more efficient for small scope

evaluations.query: Run a full security audit on our monorepo that has a Python FastAPI backend in services/api/, a TypeScript Next.js frontend in apps/web/, a Go CLI tool in tools/cli/, and shared Protobuf definitions in proto/.
expected_behavior: Scans all language-specific directories rather than only analyzing one language
Applies language-appropriate security checks: SQL injection for Python, XSS for TypeScript, memory safety for Go
Checks cross-boundary risks such as shared proto definitions and API contract mismatches
Reports findings grouped by service or language to aid triage by different teams
Does not skip a language directory due to unfamiliarity or token budget limitations without disclosing the gap

evaluations.query: Audit this codebase for prompt injection vulnerabilities, including in any AI skill definitions or hook configurations that could be manipulated by untrusted input.
expected_behavior: Examines skill SKILL.md files and hook configurations for potential prompt injection surfaces
Checks whether user-supplied input flows into skill prompts or hook parameters without sanitization
Identifies any hooks that execute shell commands with unsanitized user input
Reports on whether AI-generated output is treated as trusted input in downstream hooks or skills
Does not exempt its own plugin files from the audit scope
